response:
THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF THE 1st CIRCLE IS r, AND EACH SUCCESSIVE CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS. THIS MEANS THAT THE RADIUS OF THE 2nd CIRCLE IS 1.1r, THE RADIUS OF THE 3rd CIRCLE IS 1.21r, AND SO ON, UNTIL THE 8TH CIRCLE. THE TOTAL AREA IS THEN:

πr² + π(1.1r)² + π(1.21r)² + π(1.331r)² + π(1.4641r)² + π(1.61051r)² + π(1.771561r)² + π(1.9487171r)²

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF A CIRCLE IS 2πR, WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE CIRCLE. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2π(1.4641r), AND THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER IS 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THEN:

(2π(1.4641r)) / 0.5 = 2.9282πr

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 29282πr

THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS πr² + π(1.1r)² + π(1.21r)² + π(1.331r)² + π(1.4641r)² + π(1.61051r)² + π(1.771561r)² + π(1.9487171r)²
